perturbation.original_text: (d) During the Restricted Period, Kennedy shall provide written notice as soon as reasonably possible (it being agreed that ninety (90) days prior written notice is acceptable) before (i) Kennedy or any of his Affiliates begin to engage in the Company Business excluding the medical device business or (ii) Kennedy or any of his Affiliates enter into a transaction to acquire the stock or substantially all of the assets of any Person that engages in the Company Business excluding the medical device business.
perturbation.changed_text: (d) Kennedy shall provide written notice as soon as reasonably possible (it being agreed that ninety (90) days prior written notice is acceptable) before Kennedy or any of his Affiliates begin to engage in any new business venture.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the clause that specifies the 'Company Business excluding the medical device business', the scope of the notice requirement becomes broader, which contradicts the implicit permission granted to Kennedy to engage in the Medical Device Business in Section 6. This contradiction could cause uncertainty about whether Kennedy needs to provide notice before engaging in activities directly related to the medical device business.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company acknowledges that Kennedy is engaged, and will continue to engage, in the manufacture and sale of medical devices (the “Medical Device Business”) utilizing processes, know-how and precision machining substantially similar to those utilized by the Company in connection with the Autocam Business. The Company agrees that nothing contained herein shall in any way limit Kennedy’s ability to engage in the Medical Device Business or to utilize such processes, know-how and precision machining in connection with such Medical Device Business.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the exclusion of the 'medical device business' from the notice requirement. The contradicted text explicitly states that Kennedy is allowed to engage in the medical device business without limitation. Therefore, the changed text contradicts the contradicted text by potentially requiring Kennedy to provide notice for activities he was previously explicitly allowed to do without limitation, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (a) Kennedy covenants and represents that Kennedy has no interest in or claim to any information, whether or not in writing, of a private, secret, or confidential nature concerning the Company or Autocam (collectively, the “Proprietary Information”). Except as may otherwise be required by law, Kennedy agrees not to, without the Company’s prior written consent, (i) disclose or transfer any Proprietary Information to any Person other than the Company or (ii) use any Proprietary Information for any unauthorized purpose, either during the term of this Agreement or the Restricted Period, unless and until such Proprietary Information has become available to the public generally without fault by Kennedy.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) Kennedy covenants and represents that Kennedy has no interest in or claim to any information, whether or not in writing, of a private, secret, or confidential nature concerning the Company or Autocam (collectively, the “Proprietary Information”). Except as may otherwise be required by law, Kennedy agrees not to, without the Company’s prior written consent, disclose or transfer any Proprietary Information to any Person other than the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause that restrict Kennedy from using any Proprietary Information for any unauthorized purpose, the effectiveness of Non-Disclosure Covenants is diminished. Because now only restricts disclosing or transfering any Proprietary Information to any Person other than the Company, but if he is using the Proprietary information, the Non-Disclosure Covenants is allowing him to do that.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Kennedy covenants and represents that Kennedy has no interest in or claim to any information, whether or not in writing, of a private, secret, or confidential nature concerning the Company or Autocam (collectively, the “Proprietary Information”). Except as may otherwise be required by law, Kennedy agrees not to, without the Company’s prior written consent, disclose or transfer any Proprietary Information to any Person other than the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricted Kennedy from using Proprietary Information for any unauthorized purpose. The changed text removes this restriction, only preventing Kennedy from disclosing or transferring the information. The contradicted text is essentially the same as the changed text. This creates a contradiction because Kennedy is now allowed to use the information for unauthorized purposes, which weakens the Non-Disclosure Covenants that was present in the original contract. The removal creates a conflict between what Kennedy is allowed to do with the Proprietary information between the original text and the changed text, which is the same as the contradicted text. According to the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction, essential language is removed, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: (c) Kennedy agrees that his obligations under Sections 2(a) and 2(b) above also extend to such types of information, know-how, records, and other tangible property of the Company’s customers, suppliers, or other third parties which may have disclosed or entrusted the same to Company or Kennedy in the course of Company’s business.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) Kennedy agrees that his obligations under Sections 2(a) above also extend to such types of information, know-how, records, and other tangible property of the Company’s customers, suppliers, or other third parties which may have disclosed or entrusted the same to Company or Kennedy in the course of Company’s business.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to Section 2(b), the obligation to deliver Records upon request or termination of the Restricted Period no longer extends to information of the Company's customers, suppliers, or other third parties. This weakens the protection of third-party information that was previously covered under the non-disclosure agreement, creating a contradiction in the intended scope of protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Kennedy agrees that the portions of all files, letters, memoranda, reports, records, data, disks, electronic storage media, sketches, drawings, notebooks, program listings, or other written, photographic, or tangible material containing Proprietary Information (collectively, “Records”), whether created by Kennedy or others, which shall come into Kennedy’s custody or possession during the Restricted Period shall be and are the exclusive property of Company to be used by Kennedy only in the performance of his or her duties for the Company. All such Records or copies thereof in Kennedy’s custody or possession shall be delivered to the Company (i) upon any request by the Company and, in any event, (ii) upon the termination of the Restricted Period. After any such delivery, Employee shall not retain any such Records, copies thereof, or any other tangible property of Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text in (c) extended Kennedy's obligations under both Sections 2(a) and 2(b) to cover information of the Company's customers, suppliers, or other third parties. The changed text removes the reference to Section 2(b), meaning that the obligation to deliver Records (defined in the Contradicted Text) upon request or termination of the Restricted Period no longer explicitly extends to information of the Company's customers, suppliers, or other third parties. This creates a direct conflict because the Contradicted Text (specifically, the obligation to deliver 'all such Records') now has a narrower scope of application due to the change in (c), making them contradictory and thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event any suit or proceeding against Kennedy to enforce any of the provisions of this Agreement or on account of any damages sustained (or alleged to have been sustained) is brought by the Company by reason of Kennedy’s violation or alleged violation of any of the provisions of this Agreement, the parties agree that, in addition to other costs and damages, all reasonable costs and attorneys’ fees incurred by the party prevailing in such action shall be paid by the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event any suit or proceeding against Kennedy to enforce any of the provisions of this Agreement or on account of any damages sustained (or alleged to have been sustained) is brought by the Company by reason of Kennedy’s violation or alleged violation of any of the provisions of this Agreement, the parties agree that, in addition to other costs and damages, costs incurred by the party prevailing in such action shall be paid by the other party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'all reasonable costs and attorneys’ fees', Kennedy is no longer responsible for attorneys’ fees incurred by the party prevailing, the agreement is now contradicting 4. Necessity of Covenants; Injunctive Relief, because without attorneys’ fees, monetary damages are not an adequate remedy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Kennedy acknowledges that a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement would give rise to irreparable harm to the Company, for which monetary damages would not be an adequate remedy, and hereby agrees that in the event of a breach or a threatened breach by Kennedy of any such obligations, the Company shall, in addition to any and all other rights and remedies that may be available to it in respect of such breach, be entitled to equitable relief, including a temporary restraining order, an injunction, specific performance, and any other relief that may be available from a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that in a suit, 'all reasonable costs and attorneys’ fees' would be paid by the losing party. The changed text removes 'all reasonable costs and attorneys’ fees', specifying only 'costs incurred by the party prevailing'. This contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which states that 'monetary damages would not be an adequate remedy' for a breach of the agreement. The explanation correctly identifies that removing the attorneys' fees makes monetary damages less adequate, creating a direct conflict and thus a strong in-text contradiction. This fits the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.
